title: euryops subcarnosus subsp. foetidus
intro: Euryops subcarnosus subsp. foetidus, a plant species within the Asteraceae family, is commonly known as the 'Stink Chrysanthemum' due to its strong, unpleasant odor. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits associated with Euryops subcarnosus subsp. foetidus include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties are typically extracted through herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions.
